verdict_summary: FIX-D-5 first-emit text-only inject did NOT render Korean APF warning bubble; Copilot displayed only native English fallback 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' RRN structural decode REMAINS ABSENT (S2 protection holds).
recommendation: Pivot to FIX-D-4 (alternative React dispatch path). FIX-D-5 hybrid approach inherits the same dispatch failure as FIX-D-2: text-only first inject still does not propagate through Copilot's stream-subscriber → React message reducer pipeline. The downstream pipeline appears to require either (a) the close frame as a signal that the message tuple is complete (but close-as-first-emit caused FIX-A close_too_early), or (b) a different injection point upstream of the StreamSubscriber. Suggested next: instrument the React dispatch path to confirm whether onmessage actually fires for the text-only first emit, or attempt FIX-D-4 (alternative payload shape — SSE-style data: line / structured event envelope matching native Copilot frames).

'Close received after close' diag NOT seen this run (was a #732 FIX-A signature). Hard reload performed via Ctrl+Shift+R; new chat (m365.cloud.microsoft/chat) used to ensure clean state.
